title: Large Order Lifecycle Diagnosis
focus_questions: Is the large order trapped, unwinding, still controlling, or likely fake?
Did the order produce a result in price, or only visual noise?

summary.thesis: Large displayed size matters only when its lifecycle and price outcome show whether it is defending, trapped, unwinding, or fake.
summary.operator_translation: Do not trade the brightness of the order; trade what happened to it when price interacted with it.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prefer this card when large-liquidity tracks, replenishment, and price outcome all matter for the current replay or live-depth review.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: large displayed size near the active decision zone. Required evidence: lifecycle state such as stayed, replenished, pulled, or got traded through, plus price outcome. Do not trade: from visibility alone or before acceptance proves trap or defense. Real trigger: a confirmed lifecycle state with price response. Failure point: the order's state is still ambiguous and price has not chosen a side.

when_relevant: A large order track remains close enough to matter.
The operator needs to know whether the order is real, trapped, unwinding, or fake.
Price interaction with the order is part of the active replay script.
required_evidence: a significant_liquidity track exists near price
the order lifecycle can be described as stayed, replenished, pulled, moved, or traded through
price response after interaction is measurable
confirmation_signals: a real defending order stays, absorbs, and produces price outcome
a trapped order gets traded through and price accepts beyond it
a spoof candidate pulls before meaningful interaction or repeatedly disappears on pressure
invalidation_signals: the lifecycle state remains ambiguous and price has not chosen a side
the order appears large but never produces any measurable price outcome
the supposed trap snaps back immediately without acceptance through the level
no_trade_conditions: Do not trust a large order only because it is bright or large on the screen.
Do not call an order trapped before price has actually accepted through it.
Do not assume fake liquidity if the order keeps staying, reloading, and producing real price rejection.

entry_archetypes.name: acceptance through failed wall
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after a previously controlling order is traded through and price accepts beyond it.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the move still snaps back under the level immediately.

entry_archetypes.name: rejection from real defense
entry_archetypes.description: Trade with the defense only when the order stays, reloads, and produces repeatable rejection.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the order keeps pulling or shifting before meaningful interaction.

management_notes: Track state transitions rather than static labels.
The same order can evolve from defending to trapped, or from relevant to irrelevant.
This card is most useful with trapped-order and live-depth presets.
review_questions: What happened to the order when price first interacted with it?
Did the order produce a result in price, or only visual noise?
Was the operator trading lifecycle evidence, or just reacting to the size display?
